---
title: "Distribution Landscape: Ridgeline Plot"
author: "Ibraheem Saqib Ellahi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
output: 
  html_document:
    theme: flatly
    toc: false
    code_folding: hide
params:
  # Master Data Path
  data_path: "../1_data/master_micro_survey.csv"
  
  # Variables
  group_var: "income_tier"
  value_var: "endline_score"
  
  # Policy-Facing Labels
  metric_name: "Endline Test Score"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  warning = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  fig.path = "../4_output/figures/",
  fig.width = 9,
  fig.height = 6, 
  fig.retina = 3
)
```

```{r load-and-prep}
library(tidyverse)
library(ggridges)

# Load data
raw_data <- read_csv(params$data_path)

# ==============================================================================
# DATA SCRUBBING & PROCESSING BLOCK
# ==============================================================================
plot_data <- raw_data %>%
  # 1. Safely drop NAs
  drop_na(!!sym(params$group_var), !!sym(params$value_var)) %>%
  
  # 2. Scrub strings and enforce numeric values
  mutate(
    Raw_Group = str_to_title(str_trim(as.character(!!sym(params$group_var)))),
    Group_Clean = str_wrap(Raw_Group, width = 15),
    Value = as.numeric(!!sym(params$value_var))
  ) %>%
  
  # 3. Density Plot Protection: Filter out groups with too few observations to draw a curve
  group_by(Group_Clean) %>%
  filter(n() >= 3) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  # 4. Sort the Y-axis dynamically based on the median value of each group
  mutate(Group = fct_reorder(as.factor(Group_Clean), Value, .fun = median, na.rm = TRUE))
```

## Macro-Level Distribution Shifts

```{r plot-ridgeline}
source("../2_R/2.0_setup_theme.R", local = TRUE)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Value, y = Group, fill = Group)) +
  
  # Draw the overlapping mountain ridges
  # scale > 1 allows the curves to overlap the row above them
  geom_density_ridges(
    scale = 1.8, 
    color = "white", 
    linewidth = 1, 
    alpha = 0.8,
    rel_min_height = 0.01 # Trims the long, flat invisible tails of the distribution
  ) +
  
  # Format the X-axis for potentially large numbers
  scale_x_continuous(labels = scales::comma, expand = c(0, 0)) +
  
  # Strip the Y-axis expansion so the mountains sit flush on the bottom line
  scale_y_discrete(expand = expansion(add = c(0.2, 1.5))) +
  
  # Apply the primary brand color to all ridges for a cohesive look
  scale_fill_manual(values = rep(unname(cerp_cols["primary"]), length(unique(plot_data$Group)))) +
  
  theme_cerp() +
  
  # Clean up visual noise
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", color = cerp_cols["dark"]),
    axis.ticks.y = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_line(color = "#E0E0E0", linewidth = 0.5)
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = paste("Population Distribution:", params$metric_name),
    subtitle = "Comparing the macro-level spread of outcomes across different subgroups.",
    x = params$metric_name,
    y = NULL,
    caption = "Generated via CERP Analytics Template"
  )
```
